key_themes: Strong FY25 revenue growth (+20%) and EBITDA expansion from Randlab integration
Cash certainty and accelerated private-sector investment in R&D/M&A
summary: Animalcare Group (ANCR.L) has seen strongly positive momentum after a recommended £235.2m cash takeover by Charterhouse Capital Partners at 336p per share (36% premium to the 15 Apr 2026 close of 247p), driving the stock up ~35% to ~333p on heavy volume.[[1]](https://www.proactiveinvestors.com/companies/news/1090665/animalcare-group-shares-rise-35-after-it-agrees-235m-pe-buyout-deal-1090665.html)[[2]](https://stockinvest.us/stock/ANCR.L) This follows robust FY25 performance with revenue of ~£89.1m (+20% YoY, in line with expectations), underlying EBITDA growth of ~50%, successful Randlab integration, higher-margin product expansion, and a strong balance sheet (net debt £9.1m, leverage <1x).[[3]](https://www.investing.com/news/stock-market-news/animalcare-group-reports-20-revenue-growth-in-fy25-93CH-4459450)[[3]](https://www.investing.com/news/stock-market-news/animalcare-group-reports-20-revenue-growth-in-fy25-93CH-4459450) The board unanimously recommends the deal, citing better scope for ambitious 2030 targets as a private company; major shareholders (including directors and Harwood ~41%) have given irrevocable undertakings. Biggest risk is deal non-completion (regulatory/FIRB approval or other conditions), though momentum and support make this appear low. Pre-deal analyst targets averaged ~323p.[[4]](https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/ANCR.L/)

recent_posts.highlight: ANCR shares rise 35% after agreeing £235m PE buyout deal at 336p (36% premium).
Another takeover at decent premium (from 247p to 336p); well-needed win for shareholders including Christopher Mills & Harwood.
Animalcare Group accepted £235.2m takeover bid; shares jumped 35%.
ANCR discussed positively in small-cap investor show alongside other ideas.
